fail_to_pass.name: trusted_providers_type_widened_to_function
fail_to_pass.description: The public `BetterAuthOptions` type for `account.accountLinking.trustedProviders` accepts BOTH a static array (the existing shape) AND a function shape that takes an optional `Request` and returns `Awaitable<string[]>` (i.e. `T[] | Promise<T[]>`). Both branches of the union are part of the option's static type — applications can assign either shape without a TypeScript error.

fail_to_pass.name: all_account_linking_callsites_use_resolved_list
fail_to_pass.description: Every existing account-linking trust check has been updated so that its decision is driven by the RESOLVED trusted-providers list (whether the implementation stores it on AuthContext, resolves it inline at the callsite via a helper, or any other valid arrangement) — not by reading `options.account.accountLinking.trustedProviders` directly. The Google One Tap plugin's link-existing-user branch and the SAML SSO callback + ACS endpoints in `@better-auth/sso` are included in this — coverage cannot stop at the OAuth-callback flow.

fail_to_pass.name: documentation_explains_dynamic_shape
fail_to_pass.description: The trustedProviders documentation explains the new dynamic-function shape so application authors know they can supply a per-request resolver. This may live in any combination of the JSDoc on the option type, the docs/content/docs/reference/options.mdx reference, or both — what matters is that a reader of the public docs/types learns that the option accepts a request-receiving async function in addition to a static array.
